title: Vintage at the Crossings Community Meeting Update
resolved_text:
Memorandum
DATE: February 18, 2026
TO: Honorable Mayor and Council
THROUGH: Jackie Bryant, City Manager
FROM: Cynthia Esparza, Director
Izabella Baumann, Senior Engagement Coordinator
DEPT: Community Engagement and Services
SUBJECT: Vintage at the Crossings Community Meeting Update
The purpose of this memo is to provide an update regarding Vintage at the Crossings Apartments, a senior housing community located at 11565 Old Virginia Road, in response to public comments raised at the January 14, 2026, Reno City Council.
The public comment focused on concerns regarding property conditions and management. Council requested a community meeting to be scheduled, along with a letter summarizing these concerns and request support from the following offices: Attorney General Ford, Senator Rosen, Senator Cortez Masto, Congressman Amodei, and Northern Nevada Legal Aid.
February 10 Community Meeting
A community meeting was held on Tuesday, February 10, 2026, from 1:00 to 2:30 p.m. in the community room at Vintage at the Crossings. The community meeting was attended by 105 individuals, including representatives from the Attorney General's Office, Senator Cortez Masto's Office, Senator Rosen's Office, Northern Nevada Legal Aid, and Council members.
The community meeting was coordinated and facilitated by the Community Engagement and Services Department, and staff from Reno Direct, Code Enforcement, Reno Fire Department's Fire Prevention division, and Housing and Neighborhood Development were present. Asset Living, who acquired FPI Property Management, and Vintage at the Crossings leadership were also present and supported resident outreach and community meeting logistics.
Meeting Summary

The community meeting included a review of previously submitted comments, opportunities for attendees to submit questions and feedback, an interactive session to share community meeting goals, and an open Q&A. Residents emphasized a desire for constructive and solution-focused dialogue.
During the community meeting, Asset Living and Community Engagement and Services Department staff addressed several items and provided updates. Discussion items also included a Code Enforcement overview of the 14-day notice process, Northern Nevada Legal Aid summarized tenant rights, and alarm protocols were addressed by the Reno Fire Department. Key concerns raised and discussed included:
• Residents smoking inside the building
• Communication channels with residents, including bulletins on site and notifications during fire alarms
• Maintenance issues and building-related requests
• Resident speeding on the property and requests for speed bumps
• Accessibility at entry doors and the desire for additional automatic doors
• Overflowing trash chutes
• Payment issues regarding rent and late fees
• Requests for additional ADA-compliant parking spaces
• Concerns of retaliation when matters are reported
Many residents also expressed approval of the Vintage at the Crossings facilities, staffing, security team, and overall living experience at Vintage at the Crossings.
Likewise, several residents reported experiencing unwelcoming behavior and intimidation from other residents and were encouraged to report any incidents to Asset Living and Vintage at the Crossings management or, if in imminent danger, to local law enforcement.
Meeting Action Items
Asset Living and Vintage at the Crossings staff have received the community meeting notes and expressed commitment to proactively addressing the identified concerns. In response to the concerns raised, Asset Living and Vintage at the Crossings staff outlined the following actions and commitments:
• Inspection of air filters and smoke alarms within the next 30 days
• Water sediment testing
• Addressing work orders within 72 hours
• Rescheduling meetings between Asset Living and Vintage at the Crossings staff and residents within two weeks
• Adding additional Asset Living and Vintage at the Crossings staff to monitor trash chutes and ensure timely trash removal
• Providing updates at an upcoming City Council meeting
Currently, there is one active service request at Vintage at the Crossings and Community Engagement and Services Department staff will continue to address matters as matters arise. Community Engagement and Services Department will also be following up with a resident to review reported outstanding service requests and provide an overview of the Reno Direct process.

fields.authors: Cynthia Esparza, Director; Izabella Baumann, Senior Engagement Coordinator
fields.tldr: This memo updates the Mayor and Council on a community meeting held February 10, 2026, at Vintage at the Crossings Apartments, a senior housing community, following public concerns raised at the January 14, 2026 City Council meeting. The meeting drew 105 attendees and addressed issues including maintenance, smoking, accessibility, trash, and retaliation fears. Property manager Asset Living committed to several corrective actions, including inspections within 30 days and addressing work orders within 72 hours. The Community Engagement and Services Department will continue monitoring outstanding service requests and follow up with residents.
fields.key_stats: 105 individuals attended the February 10 community meeting; 1 active service request currently open at Vintage at the Crossings; Asset Living committed to addressing work orders within 72 hours, completing air filter and smoke alarm inspections within 30 days, and rescheduling resident meetings within two weeks.
fields.key_decisions: Council requested a community meeting and a letter summarizing resident concerns to be sent to Attorney General Ford, Senator Rosen, Senator Cortez Masto, Congressman Amodei, and Northern Nevada Legal Aid following public comments at the January 14, 2026 City Council meeting.
fields.action_items: Asset Living/Vintage at the Crossings: inspect air filters and smoke alarms within 30 days; conduct water sediment testing; address work orders within 72 hours; reschedule resident meetings within two weeks; add staff to monitor trash chutes; provide updates at an upcoming City Council meeting. Community Engagement and Services Department: follow up with a resident on outstanding service requests and provide an overview of the Reno Direct process; continue to address matters as they arise.
